review_title: Polypane is a missing tool for frontend developers
description: I'm excited about its progress, I prefer learning to use it more, I think it's a tool that frontend developers desperately needed. Very quickly it helped me uncover a bug I overlooked on tablet viewport simply because I had the pane open, it made me think when I would've uncovered it otherwise. It also helped me very with remote debugging on Safari v16 via public portal, I wouldn't have been able to build a solution that easily otherwise. I currently have some troubles with Polypane seemingly running out of memory and recently had issues with it using up much of my network, but I think the network problem has been solved.
author: Matija M.
pros: - overwhelming amount of development insights - ability to emulatione just about anything - remote debugging via public portals - incredibly responsive friendly, allowing me to create panes from autodetected breakpoints - it seems to include much of auditing that we have tools for online, and some that we didn't know, so we no longer have to chase around many of those tools - smart unified Elements and Console - fast and friendly support from the creator himself - feeling like I barely scratched the surface of what Polypane can do
cons: - learning curve, figuring out exactly what Polypane is first, and that references are best looked up in the in-panel browser - inability to inspect iframes with Polypane's inspect (I can use Chromium DevTools for that), I don't need to inspect contents, just iframe elements themselves, but that seems to be spotty - low performance of some pieces of the browser itself, e.g. it takes Capo.js a long time to expand and collapse
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Polypane is a wonderful tool that is beautifully crafted. I enjoy using it and it helps me tremendously.
author: Brady H.
pros: Powerful yet intuitive toolset. Low learning curve, up and running in minutes. Great marketing and communication by the developer.
cons: Found a feature with initially confusing behavior (pane sizes in "Focus" mode); found a feature that stalls with too much data (large screenshots); sent constructive feedback to the developer.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Needs a better Getting Started guide
author: Ryan Noble C.
pros: Free for users that had a GitHub Education account.
cons: Incredibly hard and confusing to use on a Windows 11 Desktop.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Could have used this for years!
description: Superb. Brilliant on-boarding. Brilliant app. Just a superb app - with superb support. [sensitive content hidden] is a star.
author: Peter B.
pros: The extensive range of features.... for the price. Just not having to continually resize the browser.
cons: Not used it long enough to find anything. I guess the speed could be addressed - but really - how may tabs/sizes do I need?!
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Polypane has saved me a lot of money and time
description: I am not a full-time website developer but it is within my responsibilities to audit and improve overall website functionality and visual performance. Polypane became my tool of choice for this as soon as it came out. The simplest way Polypane is saving me money is when I need to evaluate a theme for a website at a marketplace like ThemeForest. I can run any theme demo via Polypane and quickly see if it could work, or is not suitable for the task — just by looking at the desktop and mobile view at the same time. Before I bought Polypane, I kept a couple of tablets and several mobile phones around just so that I could put a design through its paces at different resolutions and see how well or poorly it performed. In the past, I have purchased many $50-70 theme only to find out later they would not work for me because the menu doesn't look well or something; with Polypane I can weed off the unsuitable themes very quickly. It also helps me a lot to communicate the work I am doing to my customers. Many people still view their own websites only on desktop and demand solutions that don't work on mobile; a simple screenshot from Polypane allows me to show them quickly where the issue lies, and propose a better solution.
author: Ivan A.
pros: Polypane is an ambitious project: it combines website design & development, functional testing and accessibility testing (these are often addressed by different tools otherwise, especially the last one). Feature development is ongoing, and valuable features are added every 3-6 months. The developer Kilian Valkhof is extremely accessible and very generous with his time. The Polypane website which he runs in parallel with the Polypane app, includes several useful tools and TONS of valuable information within the blog section.
cons: With 2-3 website tabs open and 4-5 emulated devices per tab, Polypane gets a bit sluggish for my old PC (4C/8T Haswell at 3.5GHz, 32GB RAM). Sometimes synced scroll will not work reliably.
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: Best Debugging Tool I ever Imagine
author: Fedor I.
pros: 10x speed up in debugging and accessibility testing
cons: Nothing is an issue so far. It would only be better if other browsers' engines were emulated
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Bryan O.
pros: I love the fact that you can see all views of the website or app being created all at once
cons: I dislike the fact that I cannot browse normally
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A must-have for responsive design and accessibility testing
description: If you work with UI and front-end development daily, it’s definitely worth considering giving it a shot. I have no idea why it's not popular but it's such a well done product.
author: Emanuele R.
pros: What I like most about Polypane is how it simplifies design testing. Also having multiple viewports side by side is a literal game changer. The built-in accessibility checks are also a great tool, it saves time compared to switching between separate tools. It's just packed with features overall.
cons: Being that it has so many features packed in, I feel the UI can be a bit "overwhelming" at first. Again, while it’s a powerful tool, it’s not exactly lightweight either
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

author: Kar Chung L.
pros: The responsive feature is so great and I also like the session manager which saves me lots of time for testing! P.S. [sensitive content hidden] is so helpful!
cons: No device frames, as it could have been more visually appealing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Essential for website quality assurance and front-end development
description: Our desks used to be full of devices – iphone, android phone, ipad, macbook and windows laptop. Refreshing all the screens to see if the client's website looked great at different viewpoints took time. Probably 40% of the build time is QA and testing. Polypane solves this problem by letting you view multiple viewports and set guides/rulers to see if everything lines up as you scroll.
author: John C.
pros: The passion of the creator and constant improvement. We mostly use this for testing website responsive viewport sizes side-by-side, but there is so much more here.
cons: The refresh icon was on the right side of the address bar (a hang over of previous browser UI), but this changed last year. The expereince is seamless coming from using F12 in Chrome.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: The best application for web developers.
description: Overall, it works perfectly and fulfills its main objective of debugging any errors.
author: Edwin R.
pros: I love its graphical interface, super easy to use. The ability to view the website on multiple devices (sizes) at the same time is fantastic.
cons: What I like the least is the performance; in some cases, it feels like it freezes for a few seconds.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

author: Charles C.
pros: Easy to access features needed for accessibility and simultaneous viewing of various screen sizes.
cons: The cost is more than I'd like to pay for the amount that I use it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Amazing and very clever tool for development and design.
author: Abdulsamad B.
pros: A use of Polypane as a developer or web designer will show you the numerous things that is achievable, making your work easy, efficient and excellent to. it is packed with features, and the browser extension that are huge time-saver. my favorite of all is the student free and the price is worth it to.
cons: I have no issues, but I'm hoping to see more advance and translation abilities especially for programs or code suggestions to.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Polypane as a next generation tool for software developers.
description: It's new and professional. I see myself using it a lot in feature.
author: Idris F.
pros: I like the responsiveness in terms of moving the pages around and especially the interface.
cons: I have not found anything discouraging about the App but I have experienced some functional inadequacies like not being able to submit a form across all screen sizes with a click from one screen resolution and struggling to find the settings icon.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Guirec L.
pros: I like how packaged the interface is like. You can have the tools in separate services but with Polypane, all is well integrated. You save time and it allow to be more focused because you don't have to switch between tools.
cons: It require a bit of a learning curve. To be effective you have to explore, try and customize the interface.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Loved so far, and looking forward to new updates and features :)
author: Henry O.
pros: It's a product made with the web developer in mind, as you navigate it you can see that it is made to make your life easier. The most attractive feature is the ability to lay the most common mobile screen resolutions, and as you develop, see the outcome result simultaneously in front of you.
cons: The overall GPU overhead as you add more and more viewports, depending on the complexity of the application could heat up your computer faster.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

description: I use Polypane for all of my web development procedures. From debugging all the way to deployment, it's been great so far. The pricing is fairly reasonable for what it's offering and my experience has been positive with the overall software.
author: Bhaskar R.
pros: The best part about Polypane is that it allowed me to seamlessly migrate to a new experience from my existing platform of development. The client login procedure is minimalistic and perfect. For web devs like me, the responsive view by default is beautiful & there are tons of features that I am sure developers will find helpful.
cons: The Linux client of the software (one I am using) is not the best performing browser at least on the Debian (x86-64) platform. Performance optimization is much required.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I started with the free trial and immediately knew it was something I needed to include in my workflow. I have a bunch of tools bookmarked to handle various tasks, but now pretty much all of these have been replaced by Polypane, built directly into the browser.
author: Ryan T.
pros: It may be cop out answer, but I like all the tools available! Multipanes to display various screen sizes, accessibility testing features, helpful SEO meta information, even just the small feature of being able to open a separate website from the devtools pane is super helpful!
cons: I have no complaints so far! I have a few ideas for feature requests, however the team behind Polypane seems great and quick to respond!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: James R.
pros: Polypane was a joy for the start. Easy navigation, start-up documents is the one thing that busy developers have to content with. Polypane takes all those situations and creates a fun, easy to use tool.
cons: Becaue of the excitement of being able to use a softare like polypane, I have not really thought about any cons..
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: I am happy to work with polypane
author: Elena V.
pros: It saves an enormous amount of time and allows you to test different devices at the same time. It offers a huge amount of information on various aspects of page development all in one place, simple, intuitive and fast.
cons: I have been using it recently. so far i like everything. i am positively impressed by the amount of information it provides.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Overall the experience is pretty good, I'd say value for money.
author: Gideon A.
pros: I liked the that I can view different screens at once, and there's a browser that comes in handy when I need to troubleshoot / read docs on a library
cons: I think it does have a learning curve, and takes some getting used to.

author: Habeeb A.
